mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to suppressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity relative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns as equity investors anticipate lower corporate earnings from demand destruction and margin compression.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite UNRATE acceleration, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer and construction demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth exceeding 1%, this indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both grow > 1% month-over-month simultaneously
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (RSAFS growth AND HOUST growth) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization. Declining INDPRO signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances despite INDPRO decline, or Rank IC remains above -0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 50K new jobs per month while UNRATE remains elevated above 7%. This interaction signals labor market slack and weak hiring momentum, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
regime_hint: labor market slack with weak hiring
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (weak PAYEMS AND high UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 2%, indicating real rates are deeply negative. This regime compresses equity risk premiums and supports equity valuations through financial repression, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances despite negative real rates, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window in negative real rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter decelerates below 0.5% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle slowdown. This always-on mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate earnings recession.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 62% of observations when GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 0.5%, or Rank IC > -0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable below 2.5% YoY. This supply-side productivity gain without inflation signals healthy real growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 0.8% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (strong INDPRO AND stable inflation) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
